comment.body:
1) QE means that the Federal Reserve is buying longer-dates bonds to try to bring down long-term yields.
2) There have been more indirect buyers at auctions, meaning more foreign buyers than usual.
3) You wrote that China could sell bonds to try to send a message to the US.
4) May 7

So, working backwards, I think you are saying that ME buyers are the indirect buyers.  Administration officials are concerned about the rising yields, so they are already acting here and there.  But the rate decision is so far away that if all hell breaks loose there will be an emergency meeting to approve QE between now and then.

comment.body:
1. QE - quantitative easing is one of the main tools to fight a debt crisis. The central bank buys government and in extreme cases even private bonds or equity to add liquidity to the financial system. Like that a mismatch between debt burden and liquidity can be eased. 
2. You said fed stepped in yesterday to buy in the auction. This is already a QE measure. So technically QE started yesterday?
3. China can bring pain to US by selling there immense bond Holdings. This would rise yields and bring US into a debt crisis where yields are to high to be served by lenders, especially if leveraged.

comment.body: China is just learning from what happened to Russia. Freezing Russian assets abroad and kicking Russia out of SWIFT bcs of Ukraine war was the dumbest thing to do, as it signals to anybody what will happen to their assets in case of a conflict. So China is dumping US treasuries for two reasons: (i) ceasing to finance US defence spending (ii) avoiding having their holdings frozen in case of a Taiwan invasion
